typologies.name: Classic Structuring / Smurfing
typologies.description: Deliberately breaking up cash deposits to stay below CTR threshold
indicators: Multiple cash deposits in range $9,000-$9,999 within 7 days
Same depositor across multiple branches or accounts
Aggregate of sub-threshold deposits exceeds $10,000
No apparent legitimate reason for split transactions
Timing: deposits on consecutive days or same day across branches

typologies.name: Funnel Account Structuring
typologies.description: Multiple individuals deposit cash into single account (funnel), followed by rapid withdrawal or wire out
indicators: More than 3 distinct individuals making cash deposits to same account within 30 days
Rapid withdrawal or outgoing wire shortly after deposits
Depositors have no documented relationship to account holder
Individual deposit amounts below CTR threshold

typologies.name: Domestic Layering via Wire Transfers
typologies.description: Rapid movement of funds through multiple domestic accounts to obscure origin
indicators: Same-day or next-day inbound and outbound wire transfers of similar amounts
Wire transfers to multiple unrelated accounts followed by re-aggregation
Round number wire amounts ($50,000, $100,000, $250,000)
No documented business purpose for wire activity
Account has no operating history prior to wire activity

typologies.name: International Layering via Offshore Transfers
typologies.description: Funds routed through offshore jurisdictions with weak AML regimes to obscure origin
indicators: Wire transfers to financial institutions in FATF grey/black list countries
Use of shell companies or nominee accounts as intermediaries
Correspondent banking chains with multiple intermediary banks
Funds return from offshore as 'loans' or 'investments'
Beneficial ownership opacity — unable to identify natural person

typologies.name: Real Estate Integration
typologies.description: Illicit funds integrated into legitimate economy through real estate purchases
indicators: Large cash purchases or cash-heavy down payments on real estate
Property purchase price significantly above market value
Rapid property resale (within 12 months) at similar or lower price
Title held by shell company with opaque ownership
No mortgage — all-cash transactions

typologies.name: Cash-Intensive Business Integration
typologies.description: Illicit cash commingled with legitimate business revenues in cash-intensive business
indicators: Cash revenues inconsistent with business type, size, or location
Revenue volumes significantly exceed industry benchmarks
Business deposits show suspiciously uniform or rounded amounts
Multiple businesses owned by same individual with overlapping cash flows
Employees or payroll inconsistent with reported revenue

typologies.name: Trade-Based Money Laundering — Mis-Invoicing
typologies.description: Value transferred across borders through over/under-invoicing in international trade
indicators: Payment amounts on letters of credit significantly above or below market price for described goods
Phantom shipments — payment made but no corresponding import/export record
Multiple invoices for same shipment
Counterparties in high-risk jurisdictions
Goods description vague or inconsistent with counterparty business type

typologies.name: Round-Tripping
typologies.description: Funds sent offshore as loans/investments then returned to appear as legitimate foreign investment
indicators: Outbound international wire followed by inbound 'foreign investment' or 'loan repayment' from same or related entity
Loan terms non-commercial (no interest, no repayment schedule)
Foreign 'investor' or 'lender' cannot be verified or has opaque ownership
Timing of round-trip within 6-18 months
Amount returned equals or approximates amount sent (minus fees)
